draft.title: ai workspace
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
ai workspace

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer immediate visual feedback over animated transitions in workspace tools
facade.label: Instant response
facade.acceptImplies: The workspace favors quick, no-lag interactions—clicks feel snappy, modals appear instantly, no easing delays
facade.rejectImplies: The workspace benefits from motion—transitions smooth the eye, animations signal state changes, interactions feel deliberate rather than jarring

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer gentle, approachable interfaces over sharp geometric precision in AI workspaces
facade.label: Soft rounded corners
facade.acceptImplies: The workspace should feel welcoming and less intimidating; curves over hard edges throughout UI elements, buttons, and containers
facade.rejectImplies: Sharp, angular, or minimal geometric design is preferred; the workspace should feel more precise, technical, and defined

evidence.hypothesis: Users prefer immediate visual feedback over animated transitions in workspace tools
evidence.implication: The workspace favors quick, no-lag interactions—clicks feel snappy, modals appear instantly, no easing delays

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer a friendly, approachable voice over corporate formality in their AI workspace interface
facade.axisTargeted: voice and personality — friendly vs professional
facade.label: Conversational tone
facade.acceptImplies: Lean toward warm, human, chat-like language in copy and interaction patterns. Natural phrasing over corporate jargon.
facade.rejectImplies: Lean toward polished, formal, business-like language. Professional distance. Command structure over suggestion.

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer quantified metrics over narrative summaries for workspace overview
facade.label: Dashboard with numbers
facade.acceptImplies: Workspace should foreground data density, live KPIs, and numerical feedback; users want to scan and compare figures at a glance
facade.rejectImplies: Workspace should prioritize narrative context, prose summaries, or story-driven insights; numbers alone feel cold or overwhelming

facade.hypothesis: Users building AI tools prefer high-contrast dark interfaces to reduce eye strain during long coding sessions
facade.label: Dark workspace
facade.acceptImplies: Explore darker palettes, high contrast, night-mode aesthetics, possibly with accent colors for UI elements
facade.rejectImplies: Test lighter or balanced palettes, possibly neutral grays, softer contrast ratios, daytime-friendly designs

facade.hypothesis: Users of AI workspaces prefer navigation patterns that combine persistent left-side structure with tabbed switching within tool areas, reducing cognitive load vs. pure sidebar hierarchy
facade.axisTargeted: Navigation architecture: lateral scrolling sidebar vs. vertical tab organization vs. hybrid sidebar+tabs
facade.label: Sidebar with tabs inside
facade.acceptImplies: User gravitates toward hybrid patterns where sidebar anchors context and tabs switch focused work modes. Suggests preference for multi-modal workflows within single views.
facade.rejectImplies: User dislikes mixing navigation patterns or finds tabs redundant alongside sidebar. Suggests preference for either pure flat sidebar OR pure tab-based switching, not both.

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal/clean aesthetics vs rich/detailed visual design for their workspace
Whether user wants tools always visible vs hidden/revealed on demand in their interface layout
Whether user responds to encouraging/motivational tone vs neutral/professional communication style
Whether user wants a calm/focused atmosphere vs energetic/stimulating workspace mood
Whether user prefers data-heavy dashboards vs simple, task-focused information display
Whether user likes smooth/animated transitions vs instant/snappy interface responses

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal/clean aesthetics vs rich/detailed visual design for their workspace
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants tools always visible vs hidden/revealed on demand in their interface layout
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to encouraging/motivational tone vs neutral/professional communication style
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants a calm/focused atmosphere vs energetic/stimulating workspace mood
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers data-heavy dashboards vs simple, task-focused information display
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user likes smooth/animated transitions vs instant/snappy interface responses
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Users expect fluid, gesture-based interactions (drag, swipe, multi-touch) over rigid click-based workflows in AI workspaces
facade.label: Drag and drop
facade.acceptImplies: Build workspace with smooth dragging interactions—cards, modules, and prompts move fluidly with momentum and snap-to-grid. Gestures feel natural, not mechanical.
facade.rejectImplies: Users prefer explicit, click-based actions and modal confirmations—drag gestures add cognitive friction. Interactions should be one-tap, intentional, instant.
